CARB-Approved Equipment

Professional OBD Testing, Right in the Cab

Our credentialed testers plug state-approved OBD devices directly into your truck's diagnostic port and capture everything CARB requires in a single visit.

  • Reads emissions system health straight from the engine
  • Snapshot captured and verified on the spot
  • Results transmitted digitally to CARB's database
The Regulation

What Is CARB & the Clean Truck Check?

Understand the rules before they cost you. Here's the California emissions program in plain trucker's English.

The California Air Resources Board

Established in 1967, CARB is the state agency that sets and enforces some of the strictest vehicle emissions standards in North America — rules so influential that other states model their own programs on them.

$0 / vehicle / day

Maximum fines for non-compliant vehicles can reach $10,000 per vehicle, per day.

$0M collected

In penalties CARB collected from violators in 2022 alone. Enforcement is real — and growing.

0+ lbs GVWR

Nearly all diesel and alternative-fuel vehicles over 14,000 lbs operating in California are covered — including out-of-state trucks that enter the state.

What compliance actually requires

  • Register in CARB's databaseEvery covered vehicle must be reported in the Clean Truck Check system (CTC-VIS), with the annual compliance fee paid per vehicle.
  • Submit vehicle documentationVINs, engine details, emissions test results, and records of any aftermarket modifications.
  • Pass periodic emissions testsCurrently twice per year for most vehicles, performed by a credentialed tester and submitted electronically to CARB.
  • Stay current for DMV registrationCompliance is checked against DMV records — falling behind can block your registration renewal.

Why it matters beyond fines

Non-compliance can trigger DMV registration holds, vehicles being pulled out of service, lost revenue from downtime, and damage to your reputation with brokers and shippers.

Meeting CARB standards keeps your trucks earning, protects air quality in the communities you drive through, and keeps regulators out of your mirrors.

Summarized from public CARB program guidance. Program details can change — we stay current so you don't have to.

Clean Truck Check — key milestones

Oct 2023

Open reporting began

The reporting database opened, and requirements for freight contractors, brokers, and applicable freight facilities kicked in.

Jan 31, 2024

First reporting deadline

Initial vehicle reporting and the first annual compliance fee came due for covered vehicles.

Jan 2025

Enforcement started

Periodic testing became mandatory — twice per year for most vehicles — with penalties for non-compliance now in effect.

2027

Testing frequency doubles

Required test frequency increases from 2 to 4 times per year per vehicle. Staying ahead gets even more valuable.

The official program timeline at a glance. Deadlines are based on your vehicle's DMV registration expiration — we'll help you figure out exactly when each truck is due.

The Cost of Waiting

What Non-Compliance Really Costs

💸

Hefty daily fines

Penalties escalate fast — up to $10,000 per vehicle per day — and blow up fleet budgets overnight.

🚫

DMV registration holds

Non-compliant trucks can be blocked from renewing registration until they're brought into compliance.

⏸️

Out-of-service downtime

Vehicles can be pulled from service — lost loads, missed contracts, and scheduling chaos.

📉

Reputation damage

Compliance issues erode trust with brokers, shippers, and customers — and invite more scrutiny.

Credentialed & Verified

Testing You Can Trust — Credentialed Under the CARB Program

Clean Truck Check tests are only valid when performed by a credentialed tester using CARB-approved equipment. We're credentialed under the program, so every inspection we perform is captured correctly and lands in CARB's database instantly.

Keep our card in your cab — one call or text and your next test is booked.
